userStories.title: DecomposeBuilder fluent API and prompt sections
userStories.description:
Create the core DecomposeBuilder class in `src/decompose/builder.ts` with fluent API following the PromptBuilder pattern.

New files:
- `src/decompose/types.ts` — DecomposeConfig, DecomposeResult, SubStory, ValidationResult types
- `src/decompose/builder.ts` — DecomposeBuilder class with `.for(story).prd(prd).codebase(ctx).config(cfg).buildPrompt()` and `.decompose(adapter)` methods
- `src/decompose/sections/target-story.ts` — builds the target story section (full story details + decompose instruction)
- `src/decompose/sections/sibling-stories.ts` — builds sibling stories section (all other PRD stories: id, title, status, AC summary for overlap prevention)
- `src/decompose/sections/codebase.ts` — builds codebase context section (reuse scanCodebase output)
- `src/decompose/sections/constraints.ts` — builds constraints section (max substories, max complexity, output JSON schema, nonOverlapJustification requirement)
- `src/decompose/sections/index.ts` — barrel export
- `src/decompose/index.ts` — barrel export

The builder composes sections with SECTION_SEP (same as PromptBuilder). The `.decompose(adapter)` method calls `adapter.decompose()` with the composed prompt, then parses and validates the result.

Parsing reuses `parseDecomposeOutput()` from `src/agents/claude-decompose.ts` — extend it to handle the substory schema (parentStoryId, nonOverlapJustification fields).

Run: NAX_SKIP_PRECHECK=1 bun test test/unit/decompose/ --timeout=60000
Commit: 'feat(decompose): add DecomposeBuilder fluent API and prompt sections'
acceptanceCriteria: DecomposeBuilder.for(story) returns builder instance
Fluent API: .prd(), .codebase(), .config() all return builder (chainable)
.buildPrompt() composes all 4 sections with SECTION_SEP separators
Target story section includes full story details (id, title, description, AC, tags, dependencies)
Sibling stories section includes all other PRD stories with id, title, status, and AC summary
Constraints section includes max substories, max complexity, JSON output schema, and nonOverlapJustification requirement
All types exported from src/decompose/index.ts
TypeScript compiles cleanly

routing.reasoning: Multi-file architectural feature following established PromptBuilder pattern; straightforward section composition and type design with clear AC, but involves 8 coordinated files and pattern application across multiple concerns.

userStories.title: Post-decompose validators
userStories.description:
Create validators that run after LLM decomposition to ensure quality.

New files:
- `src/decompose/validators/overlap.ts` — keyword + acceptance-criteria similarity check between each substory and all existing PRD stories. Uses normalized keyword intersection (Jaccard-like). Flag pairs with similarity > 0.6 as warnings, > 0.8 as errors.
- `src/decompose/validators/coverage.ts` — checks that the union of substory acceptance criteria covers the original story's AC. Uses keyword matching to map each original AC to at least one substory AC. Warns on unmatched original criteria.
- `src/decompose/validators/complexity.ts` — validates each substory complexity is <= config.maxSubstoryComplexity (default: medium). Reuses classifyComplexity() from src/routing/router.ts as a cross-check against LLM-assigned complexity.
- `src/decompose/validators/dependency.ts` — validates no circular dependencies among substories, all referenced dependency IDs exist (in substories or existing PRD), no ID collisions with existing PRD story IDs.
- `src/decompose/validators/index.ts` — runAllValidators() function that runs all validators in sequence and returns merged ValidationResult.

ValidationResult type: { valid: boolean; errors: string[]; warnings: string[] }

Integrate into DecomposeBuilder.decompose() — after parsing, run validators. If valid=false and retries remaining, re-prompt LLM with error feedback. If retries exhausted, return result with errors for caller to handle.

Run: NAX_SKIP_PRECHECK=1 bun test test/unit/decompose/ --timeout=60000
Commit: 'feat(decompose): add post-decompose validators (overlap, coverage, complexity, dependency)'
acceptanceCriteria: Overlap validator detects >0.6 keyword similarity between substory and existing story
Coverage validator warns when original AC has no matching substory AC
Complexity validator rejects substories exceeding maxSubstoryComplexity
Dependency validator detects circular dependencies among substories
Dependency validator rejects references to non-existent story IDs
ID collision validator rejects substory IDs that collide with existing PRD IDs
runAllValidators() returns merged ValidationResult with all errors and warnings
DecomposeBuilder.decompose() retries on validation failure up to config.maxRetries

userStories.title: Config schema, PRD mutation, and story-oversized trigger
userStories.description:
Wire decomposition into config, PRD lifecycle, and interaction system.

1. Config schema changes:
- Add DecomposeConfig to src/config/types.ts: { trigger: 'auto'|'confirm'|'disabled', maxAcceptanceCriteria: number (default 6), maxSubstories: number (default 5), maxSubstoryComplexity: Complexity (default 'medium'), maxRetries: number (default 2), model: ModelTier (default 'balanced') }
- Add `decompose: DecomposeConfig` to NaxConfig
- Add defaults to src/config/schema.ts
- Add decompose section to `nax config --explain`

2. PRD mutation:
- Add 'decomposed' to StoryStatus union in src/prd/types.ts
- Create src/decompose/apply.ts — applyDecomposition(prd, result): marks original story as 'decomposed', inserts substories after original with status 'pending' and parentStoryId reference, re-routes each substory via routeTask()
- Update getNextStory() to skip 'decomposed' stories (same as 'skipped')
- Update countStories() to include decomposed count

3. Interaction trigger:
- Add 'story-oversized' to TriggerName union in src/interaction/types.ts
- Add metadata to TRIGGER_METADATA: { defaultFallback: 'continue', safety: 'yellow', defaultSummary: 'Story {{storyId}} is oversized ({{criteriaCount}} acceptance criteria) — decompose into smaller stories?' }
- Add checkStoryOversized() to src/interaction/triggers.ts

Run: NAX_SKIP_PRECHECK=1 bun test test/unit/decompose/ test/unit/prd/ test/unit/config/ --timeout=60000
Commit: 'feat(decompose): add config schema, PRD mutation, and story-oversized trigger'
acceptanceCriteria: DecomposeConfig added to NaxConfig with all fields and defaults
nax config --explain includes decompose section
'decomposed' is a valid StoryStatus value
applyDecomposition() marks original as decomposed, inserts substories with parentStoryId
getNextStory() skips decomposed stories
countStories() includes decomposed count
'story-oversized' trigger registered in TRIGGER_METADATA
checkStoryOversized() fires trigger and returns decompose/skip/continue action

userStories.title: Pipeline integration and CLI entry point
userStories.description:
Wire DecomposeBuilder into the pipeline routing stage and CLI.

1. Pipeline integration:
- In src/pipeline/stages/routing.ts (or the appropriate stage): after classifyComplexity(), check if story exceeds config.decompose.maxAcceptanceCriteria AND complexity is complex/expert. If yes:
  - If config.decompose.trigger === 'disabled': log warning, continue with original story
  - If config.decompose.trigger === 'confirm': fire 'story-oversized' interaction trigger. On approve → decompose. On reject → continue with original.
  - If config.decompose.trigger === 'auto': decompose directly without interaction
- On successful decomposition: call applyDecomposition(prd, result), savePRD(), then restart story iteration from the first new substory
- On failed decomposition (validators failed after retries): log warning, continue with original story

2. CLI entry point:
- Add --decompose <storyId> flag to nax analyse command in src/commands/analyze.ts or src/cli/analyze.ts
- When --decompose is passed: loadPRD(), find story by ID, run DecomposeBuilder.for(story).prd(prd).codebase(scanCodebase).config(config.decompose).decompose(adapter), validate, apply, savePRD(), print summary table
- Add --decompose-oversized flag: iterate all stories, decompose any that exceed threshold

Run: NAX_SKIP_PRECHECK=1 bun test test/unit/decompose/ test/unit/pipeline/ --timeout=60000
Commit: 'feat(decompose): wire pipeline integration and CLI entry point'
acceptanceCriteria: Routing stage detects oversized stories based on config threshold
confirm mode fires story-oversized trigger and respects response
auto mode decomposes without interaction prompt
disabled mode logs warning and continues
Successful decomposition applies to PRD and saves
Failed decomposition (after retries) falls back to original story with warning
nax analyse --decompose US-003 decomposes a specific story and saves PRD
nax analyse --decompose-oversized bulk-decomposes all oversized stories
CLI prints summary table of decomposed substories
